    Global Search Dialog
      1. About RWJF
      2. How We Work

      Health Equity Narrative House

      Building A Transformative Narrative For Health Equity
      Sep-05-2024 1-min read

       

      The “Health Equity Narrative House” is a powerful tool that suggests the narratives we need to create, amplify, and organize around in order to foster a healthier, fairer, and more just society.

      The Narrative House can be used for framing, storytelling, organizing, and grantmaking. It aims to support a larger and stronger “base and choir” who actively work to advance a society where health is no longer a privilege, but a right.

       

      Are you ready to see how you can implement the Health Equity Narrative House today? 

      Start by downloading the Action Guide for an overview of the Narrative House, including ideas for using it in your work, as well as the Build Your Narrative worksheet to share about how you're helping build a world where health is no longer a privilege, but a right. 

      Access the Action Guide PDF
      Access the Build Your Narrative worksheet

       

      Dig in deeper by downloading the full Health Equity Narrative House guide—a more detailed look at what each of the parts of the house mean; the theory of change and process behind the creation of the house; a deeper look at what the house is intended to be used for, and critically, what it isn’t; and ideas for future research and testing.

      Access the Narrative House PDF

       

      Use these tools to turn your story into action. Leaders and influencers are already driving change. Say it louder, clearer, and in your own voice.

      Access resources to drive change

      Share your email for an update

      We will send you an abbreviated user guide for the narrative house, and provide the opportunity to share your feedback.

      Register

      Are you an RWJF Grantee (Past or Present?)
      Are you a European Union Resident?
      Tell us what type of content you want to receive.

      Get informed: Receive the latest news, research, and funding opportunities from RWJF each week.

      Get funded by RWJF: Receive notifications when new funding opportunities are released.

      Stay up to date: Receive monthly updates on RWJF-sponsored research that informs leading health policy debates on Capitol Hill - from health equity to improving access to quality health care, child nutrition, equitable housing, and more.

      Shop talk for researchers. This monthly newsletter covers research news and opportunities from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation.

      Communications expedite action. Get periodic research and expert insights on the best ways to communicate so we can spread what works.

      In addition to any other subscriptions you choose, we will send you other announcements, news, and opportunities that we think might be of interest to you. If you have indicated that you are an European Union resident, we will send these communications to you only if you choose to receive them by checking this box.

      Yes! I want to tell you more about myself and receive personalized communications in the future.

      Which profession or pursuit best describes you?

      Are you an RWJF Grantee (Past or Present?)
      Are you a European Union Resident?
      Tell us what type of content you want to receive. Select at least one (required).

      Be informed with our twice a month newsletter updating you with relevant news and research around a Culture of Health, as well as the latest funding opportunities.

      Get funded by RWJF: Receive notifications when new funding opportunities are released.

      Receive monthly updates on RWJF-sponsored research that informs many robust health policy debates on Capitol Hill, covering topics like health equity, improving access to quality healthcare, equitable housing, and more.

      Shop talk for researchers. This monthly newsletter covers research news and opportunities from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation.

      Communications expedite action. Get periodic research and expert insights on the best ways to communicate so we can spread what works.

      Sometimes we have news, announcements or opportunities that don't quite fit the subscription parameters above. If you're interested, we'll send you this information under "There's more...". *If you've indicated you are an EU resident, we will only send these communications if you intentionally check this box.
